qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Consider the grammar S A Construct an equivalent grammar with no left recursion and with minimum number of production rules.

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: A grammar that is both left and right recursive for a non-terminal, is A. Ambiguous B. Unambiguous C. Information is not sufficient to decide whether it is ambiguous or unambiguous D. None of the above

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: Which of the following statements is false? A. An unambiguous grammar has same leftmost and rightmost derivation B. An LL(1) parser is a top-down parser C. LALR is more powerful than SLR D. An ambiguous grammar can never be LR(k) for any k

qids.subject: Compiler Design
qids.prose: A. Remove left-recursion from the following grammar: B. Consider the following grammar: Construct all possible parse trees for the string abab. Is the grammar ambiguous?
